title: senecio harveyanus
intro: Senecio harveyanus, also known as Harvey's groundsel, is a shrubby plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the daisy family and has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant's medicinal properties are said to help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as provide relief from muscle spasms and digestive problems. Senecio harveyanus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
